Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Undroidwish – A single-file, batteries-included Tcl/Tk binary for many platforms 73 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. The ANSI art "telecomics" of the 1992 election 34 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. OpenBSD on Motorola 88000 Processors 86 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. A Verilog to Factorio Compiler and Simulator (Working RISC-V CPU) 62 upvotes, 6 comments
  5. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 387 upvotes, 66 comments
  6. Cat Itecture: Better Cat Window Boxes (2023) 53 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 965 upvotes, 204 comments
  8. CSS is DOOMed 307 upvotes, 67 comments
  9. Linux is an interpreter 195 upvotes, 42 comments
  10. Detecting file changes on macOS with kqueue 80 upvotes, 15 comments
  11. The 667MHz Machine 49 upvotes, 8 comments
  12. Spanish legislation as a Git repo 726 upvotes, 218 comments
  13. Improved Git Diffs with Delta, Fzf and a Little Shell Scripting 146 upvotes, 36 comments
  14. OpenCiv1 – open-source rewrite of Civ1 137 upvotes, 38 comments
  15. The road to electric – in charts and data [UK] 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. I decompiled the White House's new app 496 upvotes, 182 comments
  17. Technology: The (nearly) perfect USB cable tester does exist 26 upvotes, 6 comments
  18. A laser-based process that enables adhesive-free paper packaging 69 upvotes, 26 comments
  19. The first 40 months of the AI era 172 upvotes, 90 comments
  20. South Korea Mandates Solar Panels for Public Parking Lots 284 upvotes, 160 comments
  21. Alzheimer's disease mortality among taxi and ambulance drivers (2024) 113 upvotes, 63 comments
  22. Further human + AI + proof assistant work on Knuth's "Claude Cycles" problem 198 upvotes, 130 comments
  23. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 629 upvotes, 473 comments
  24. What if AI doesn't need more RAM but better math? 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. The case for becoming a manager 34 upvotes, 21 comments
  26. Modeling what makes paper-folding puzzles hard 22 upvotes, 12 comments
  27. 1929: Inside the Greatest Crash in Wall Street History 69 upvotes, 59 comments
  28. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 12 upvotes, 6 comments
  29. Android’s new sideload settings will carry over to new devices 84 upvotes, 109 comments
  30. InpharmD (YC W21) Is Hiring – Senior Ruby on Rails Developer 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 4:30pm, 29 March 2026. Source here.
